This is similar to Unique Paths, but you need to handle obstacles. What should dp[i][j] be if there's an obstacle at (i, j)?
If obstacleGrid[i][j] == 1, set dp[i][j] = 0. Otherwise, dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1].
Be careful with the first row and first column: once you hit an obstacle, all subsequent cells in that row/column have 0 paths.
Check edge cases: obstacle at start (0,0) or end (m-1,n-1) means 0 paths.
